message:
Add additional GraphemePool tests and methods

Expand test coverage and utility methods for GraphemePool.
Includes routine beads metadata updates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1ob","title":"demo_showcase runtime: resilience + capability gating implementation","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Resilience + Capability Gating Implementation

## Purpose

The demo must look great on ideal terminals, but it must also be robust when:

- terminal is too small
- true color is not supported
- hyperlinks are unsupported
- mouse events aren’t available
- stdout/stderr aren’t TTYs

This bead implements the degradation rules defined in `bd-1i7`.

## Behaviors

### Small Terminal

- Define a minimum supported size (from spec).
- Below that:
  - switch to a compact layout (single column or stacked panels)
  - reduce visual density (fewer borders, smaller headers)
  - ensure no panics / out-of-bounds due to rect math

### Missing Capabilities

- If no truecolor: use a reduced palette (256-color-safe or basic).
- If no hyperlinks: render link text without OSC 8 and add a `(copy)` hint.
- If no mouse: hide click hints, ensure keyboard-only navigation covers everything.
- If focus events not available: don’t depend on them for pause behavior.

### Non‑TTY

- Provide a safe fallback:
  - `--headless-smoke` should work without a TTY.
  - If run interactively without TTY and not headless, print a one-line guidance message and exit with non-zero.

## Capability Override (Testability)

To make resilience testable and debuggable, the demo supports an optional capability preset override:

- `--cap-preset <name>` (see `bd-2iv`)

Rules:

- Interactive (TTY): presets can only DISABLE capabilities (effective = detected ∩ preset).
- Headless: presets define the effective caps directly.

The UI should surface effective capabilities (inspector + status hints) so the viewer understands why a feature is disabled.

This is exercised by the degradation matrix suite (`bd-2bnv`).

## Acceptance Notes

- The demo never panics due to resize or missing terminal features.
- UI clearly indicates when features are disabled (subtle warning in status/inspector).
- Degradation behavior is consistent and snapshottable in headless mode.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- The demo never panics due to missing capabilities or resize/relayout.
- Below minimum size, compact layout is activated and remains usable (help/quit always visible).
- When features are disabled (no mouse/links/truecolor/sync), the UI degrades gracefully and indicates the limitation.
- `--cap-preset` only disables capabilities in interactive mode (effective = detected ∩ preset) and defines caps in headless mode.
- The degradation matrix suite (`bd-2bnv`) passes and provides ongoing regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:49.434807357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:34:31.497181251Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:34:31.497040558Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:10.635614919Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-1xsw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:32:16.340490585Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:49.450381440Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:11.169561512Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-3ii","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:11.747840355Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-ix0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:12.288427025Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-pnln","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:55:18.077506237Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ok","title":"demo_showcase spec: visual design system (palette, typography, spacing, components)","description":"# demo_showcase Spec — Visual Design System

## Goal

A consistent, modern visual language that makes OpenTUI feel premium:

- strong spacing rhythm
- crisp borders
- tasteful gradients
- glassy overlays
- clear focus/selection

This spec defines **multiple themes** because the demo explicitly showcases theme switching (UI + highlighting) as a real app capability.

## Theme Set

### Theme A: “Synthwave Professional” (Default, Dark)

Base background:
- BG0 = #0f1220 (app background)
- BG1 = #151a2e (panel background)
- BG2 = #1d2440 (raised surfaces / cards)

Text:
- FG0 = #e6e6e6 (primary)
- FG1 = #aeb6d6 (secondary)
- FG2 = #6c7396 (dim)

Accents:
- Aqua   = #4dd6ff
- Pink   = #ff4fd8
- Green  = #2bff88
- Orange = #ffb020
- Red    = #ff4455

Selection + Focus:
- selection bg: #2a335c (typically applied as a low-alpha overlay)
- focus border: Aqua (and bold)

### Theme B: “Light (Paper)”

Base background:
- BG0 = #f7f7fb
- BG1 = #ffffff
- BG2 = #eef0f7

Text:
- FG0 = #1a1b26
- FG1 = #3a3f5a
- FG2 = #6a6f8a

Accents:
- Blue   = #2a6fff
- Purple = #7b61ff
- Green  = #00a86b
- Orange = #ff8a00
- Red    = #e53935

Selection + Focus:
- selection bg: #dbe6ff (low alpha)
- focus border: Blue

### Theme C: “Solarized-ish” (Low Eye Strain)

Base background:
- BG0 = #002b36
- BG1 = #073642
- BG2 = #0b4452

Text:
- FG0 = #eee8d5
- FG1 = #93a1a1
- FG2 = #657b83

Accents:
- Cyan   = #2aa198
- Yellow = #b58900
- Orange = #cb4b16
- Red    = #dc322f
- Blue   = #268bd2

Selection + Focus:
- selection bg: #0d5161 (low alpha)
- focus border: Cyan

### Theme D: “High Contrast” (Accessibility / Worst-Case Terminals)

Base background:
- BG0 = #000000
- BG1 = #000000
- BG2 = #111111

Text:
- FG0 = #ffffff
- FG1 = #e0e0e0
- FG2 = #a0a0a0

Accents:
- Cyan   = #00ffff
- Yellow = #ffff00
- Magenta= #ff00ff
- Green  = #00ff00
- Red    = #ff0000

Selection + Focus:
- selection bg: #333333
- focus border: Yellow

## Component Styling Tokens (Theme-Relative)

- Panel border: dim FG2
- Focused panel border: focus accent + bold + optional 1px “glow line”
- Headers: gradient bar + bold title
- Status bar: BG2 with muted text; hotkeys in FG0 bold
- Toasts: rounded box + opacity stack (glass)

## Typography / Characters

- Prefer Unicode box drawing (rounded/heavy where appropriate).
- Use subtle separators: │, ─, ·.
- Keep iconography mostly to single-codepoint symbols for stable width.

## Gradient Rules

- Gradients should be subtle: 5–10% perceived change across width.
- Use Rgba::lerp between two close hues.

## Theme Switching Rules

- Theme switching changes BOTH:
  1) UI palette/token set
  2) syntax highlighting theme (Theme::dark/light/…)

The demo should present theme switching as a real workflow feature:

- via command palette
- optionally via number keys when help is open (discoverable)

## Degradation Rules (Terminal Capability Variance)

- If no truecolor, map theme colors to the closest 256-color approximation.
- Avoid relying on italics/strikethrough; treat as “bonus” if supported.

## What This Bead Produces

- Canonical theme token sets used everywhere in the demo.
- A single “current theme” selection mechanism shared by chrome, panels, overlays, toasts, and highlighting.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Palette tokens defined (BG/FG/Accents)
- [ ] Focus/selection styling rules defined
- [ ] Component styling rules defined (borders, headers, status, toasts)
- [ ] Gradient rules defined
- [ ] Degradation rules defined","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:10.420443553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:18:02.358210255Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:18:02.358144072Z","close_reason":"Completed: Created docs/DEMO_SHOWCASE_DESIGN_SYSTEM.md with 4 theme definitions, component styling, typography, spacing, gradients, and degradation rules","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ok","depends_on_id":"bd-2jc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:10.450428345Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ok","depends_on_id":"bd-2mu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:58.107964883Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Function Signature (Rust)
```rust
pub fn clone_batch(&mut self, ids: &[u32])
```

## Purpose
Increments reference counts for multiple IDs. Used when copying regions between buffers.

## Implementation Notes
- Increment ref count for each valid ID
- Skip ID 0 and invalid IDs
- Useful for buffer copy operations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Increments all valid refs
- [ ] Handles invalid IDs
- [ ] Handles ID 0

## Testing
- Clone batch of valid IDs
- Clone with mixed valid/invalid
- Verify ref counts increased","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:36:22.167076690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:29:05.980692419Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1p6l","depends_on_id":"bd-g3az","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:36:22.180294042Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1pa8","title":"Fix over-permissive terminal capability defaults","description":"## Problem

src/terminal/capabilities.rs lines 59-81 defaults to very permissive capabilities:

```rust
impl Default for Capabilities {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            color: ColorSupport::TrueColor,  // Assumes best case!
            unicode: true,
            mouse: true,
            focus: true,
            bracketed_paste: true,
            // ... all enabled
        }
    }
}
```

## Impact

If capability detection fails (e.g., broken TERM variable, unusual terminal):
- Code assumes TrueColor support
- Sends true color escape sequences to terminal that doesn't support them
- Terminal displays garbage or ignores sequences
- User sees corrupted output

## Better Approach

Default to minimal capabilities and only enable after confirmation:

```rust
impl Default for Capabilities {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            color: ColorSupport::Basic,  // Conservative default
            unicode: false,  // Don't assume
            mouse: false,
            focus: false,
            bracketed_paste: false,
            // ... minimal defaults
        }
    }
}
```

Then in detect():
```rust
pub fn detect() -> Self {
    let mut caps = Self::default();  // Start minimal
    
    // Enable features based on detection
    if has_true_color_env() {
        caps.color = ColorSupport::TrueColor;
    }
    // ...
}
```

## Trade-off

Conservative defaults mean:
- Basic terminals work correctly out of the box
- Advanced terminals get detected features
- But if detection fails, features are disabled (not broken)

## Files to Modify

- src/terminal/capabilities.rs: Default impl, detect() method

## Testing

1. Test with missing TERM variable
2. Test with invalid TERM variable
3. Verify conservative defaults are used","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T23:11:03.225484592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:11:03.380028397Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:11:03.379963616Z","close_reason":"Fixed: Changed Default impl to use conservative defaults (Basic color, no unicode, all advanced features disabled). Added is_xterm_compatible() to detect xterm-compatible terminals. detect() now starts conservative and enables features based on TERM value. Updated tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pa8","depends_on_id":"bd-2qib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T23:11:03.238695079Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1pd","title":"demo_showcase UI: implement design system (palette + style helpers + gradients)","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Implement Design System

## Purpose

Turn the design system spec (`bd-1ok`) into code inside the demo binary:

- theme token sets (multiple themes)
- color tokens (`Rgba` constants)
- style builders (header style, border style, focus style, selection style)
- gradient helpers using `Rgba::lerp`

## Implementation Pattern

Keep it self-contained inside `src/bin/demo_showcase.rs`:

- `enum UiTheme { SynthwaveDark, PaperLight, Solarized, HighContrast }`
- `struct Tokens { bg0, bg1, bg2, fg0, fg1, fg2, accent_primary, ... }`
- `impl UiTheme { fn tokens(self, caps: &Capabilities) -> Tokens }`

No file proliferation unless truly necessary.

## Highlight Theme Mapping

Theme switching should update BOTH:

1) UI palette tokens
2) syntax highlighting theme used by the editor panel

Current highlight themes available in the library are:

- Theme::dark()
- Theme::light()
- Theme::high_contrast()

So the mapping should be:

- UiTheme::SynthwaveDark -> Theme::dark()
- UiTheme::PaperLight -> Theme::light()
- UiTheme::Solarized -> Theme::dark() (closest available unless we add a Solarized highlight theme later)
- UiTheme::HighContrast -> Theme::high_contrast()

## Must Demonstrate

- Truecolor gradients when available (still okay if approximated)
- cohesive spacing and hierarchy
- strong focus/selection affordances that remain readable in reduced-color terminals

Dependencies:
- This bead is blocked on the design system spec.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Palette constants exist and match spec
- [ ] Style helpers exist (borders, focus, headers, status, toasts)
- [ ] Gradient helper(s) exist and are used in top bar or background","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"RusticSnow","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:42.059622696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:13:06.238177379Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:13:06.238002503Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pd","depends_on_id":"bd-1ok","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:49.547954895Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1pd","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:42.071894164Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1q0f","title":"EPIC: Event System - Complete API Parity","description":"# Event System Module API Parity

## Zig Reference: Section 15 of EXISTING_OPENTUI_STRUCTURE.md

## Missing Components

### Event Bus
The Zig implementation has a global event dispatcher for terminal events:
- `setEventCallback(fn(name, data))` - Register event handler
- Fires events for terminal responses
- Used for async capability detection

### Logger
Debug logging system with levels:
- `setLogCallback(fn(level, msg))` - Register log handler
- Levels: debug, info, warn, error
- Used throughout the library for diagnostics

## Current Rust State
Need to check if event/logging exists. May need:
- Event trait/callback system
- Log integration (consider tracing crate)
- Terminal response event emission

## Design Considerations
- Should integrate with Rust ecosystem (tracing, log crates)
- Consider async-friendly design
- Event callbacks should be Send + Sync for threading

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Event callback registration
- [ ] Terminal response events
- [ ] Log level filtering
- [ ] Thread-safe event dispatch","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:17:15.145501166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:17:15.154879861Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1q0f","depends_on_id":"bd-3fx7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:17:15.154828576Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-219","title":"Fix Bracketed Paste Mode Bug","description":"## Overview
Fix a bug in the existing input parser where bracketed paste mode (ESC[200~ ... ESC[201~) doesn't work correctly.

## Current State
The parser already handles:
- CSI sequences (cursor keys, function keys, modifiers) ✓
- SS3 sequences (alternate cursor key format) ✓
- Focus events (ESC[I, ESC[O) ✓
- UTF-8 character sequences ✓
- SGR and X11 mouse parsing ✓
- Partial sequence handling ✓

## Bug Description
In `src/input/parser.rs` at lines 254-256, when the parser encounters CSI 200~ (bracketed paste start sequence), it incorrectly returns `Err(ParseError::Incomplete)` instead of setting `self.in_paste = true` to enter paste mode.

```rust
// CURRENT (BROKEN):
200 => {
    // Bracketed paste start - handled elsewhere
    return Err(ParseError::Incomplete);
}

// SHOULD BE:
200 => {
    self.in_paste = true;
    return Err(ParseError::Incomplete); // Need more data for paste content
}
```

The `parse_paste()` method at line 368 correctly handles collecting paste content and detecting the end sequence (ESC[201~), but the paste mode is never entered because `in_paste` is never set to `true`.

## Files to Modify
- src/input/parser.rs - Fix the CSI 200~ handling to set `in_paste = true`

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (add to existing tests in parser.rs)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_parse_bracketed_paste_simple() {
    let mut parser = InputParser::new();
    // Start paste, content, end paste
    let input = b"\x1b[200~Hello, World!\x1b[201~";
    eprintln!("[TEST] Input: {:?}", input);
    eprintln!("[TEST] Hex: {}", input.iter().map(|b| format!("{:02x}", b)).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(" "));
    
    let (event, consumed) = parser.parse(input).unwrap();
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Event: {:?}", event);
    eprintln!("[TEST] Consumed: {} bytes", consumed);
    
    match event {
        Event::Paste(paste) => {
            eprintln!("[TEST] Paste content: {:?}", paste.content());
            assert_eq!(paste.content(), "Hello, World!");
        }
        other => panic!("Expected Paste event, got {:?}", other),
    }
    
    assert_eq!(consumed, input.len());
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: Simple bracketed paste works");
}

#[test]
fn test_parse_bracketed_paste_with_newlines() {
    let mut parser = InputParser::new();
    let input = b"\x1b[200~Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3\x1b[201~";
    eprintln!("[TEST] Testing paste with newlines");
    
    let (event, _) = parser.parse(input).unwrap();
    
    match event {
        Event::Paste(paste) => {
            let content = paste.content();
            eprintln!("[TEST] Paste content: {:?}", content);
            assert!(content.contains('\n'), "Paste should preserve newlines");
            assert_eq!(content.lines().count(), 3);
        }
        _ => panic!("Expected Paste event"),
    }
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: Newlines preserved in paste");
}

#[test]
fn test_parse_bracketed_paste_utf8() {
    let mut parser = InputParser::new();
    let input = "Hello, 世界! 🎉".as_bytes();
    let mut full_input = vec![];
    full_input.extend_from_slice(b"\x1b[200~");
    full_input.extend_from_slice(input);
    full_input.extend_from_slice(b"\x1b[201~");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Testing UTF-8 paste: {:?}", std::str::from_utf8(input).unwrap());
    
    let (event, _) = parser.parse(&full_input).unwrap();
    
    match event {
        Event::Paste(paste) => {
            eprintln!("[TEST] Paste content: {:?}", paste.content());
            assert!(paste.content().contains("世界"));
            assert!(paste.content().contains("🎉"));
        }
        _ => panic!("Expected Paste event"),
    }
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: UTF-8 content preserved in paste");
}

#[test]
fn test_parse_bracketed_paste_incremental() {
    let mut parser = InputParser::new();
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Testing incremental paste parsing");
    
    // Send start sequence
    let result = parser.parse(b"\x1b[200~");
    eprintln!("[TEST] After start sequence: {:?}", result);
    assert!(result.is_err(), "Should need more data");
    
    // Send partial content (no end yet)
    let result = parser.parse(b"Hello");
    eprintln!("[TEST] After partial content: {:?}", result);
    assert!(result.is_err(), "Should still need end sequence");
    
    // Send end sequence
    let result = parser.parse(b"\x1b[201~");
    eprintln!("[TEST] After end sequence: {:?}", result);
    // Note: actual behavior depends on implementation
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: Incremental paste handled");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CSI 200~ (paste start) correctly sets `in_paste = true`
- [ ] Paste content is collected until ESC[201~ is received
- [ ] Paste(PasteEvent) is returned with correct content
- [ ] UTF-8 content in paste is preserved correctly
- [ ] Newlines in paste content are preserved
- [ ] Incremental parsing works (partial paste buffers)
- [ ] All 4 new unit tests pass with detailed logging
- [ ] Existing parser tests still pass (no regression)","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Parser handles all xterm-compatible key sequences
- [ ] UTF-8 characters parse correctly (including 4-byte emoji)
- [ ] Partial sequences return NeedMoreData (not error)
- [ ] Invalid sequences return Unknown (not panic)
- [ ] Performance: parse 100K events/sec
- [ ] All 18+ unit tests pass
- [ ] E2E test with recorded sequences passes
- [ ] No clippy warnings

Comprehensive tests exist and pass (7 bracketed paste tests). Verified with cargo test --nocapture.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-21ap","title":"EPIC: TextBuffer Module - Complete API Parity","description":"# TextBuffer Module API Parity

## Current Coverage: ~80%

## Missing Functions (11 total)

### Segment API
1. **set_segment_style(seg_id, style)** - Set style for text segment
2. **get_segment_style(seg_id)** - Get style for segment
3. **iter_segments()** - Iterate over all segments
4. **iter_segments_in_range(start, end)** - Iterate segments in byte range
5. **remove_segment(seg_id)** - Remove a segment by ID
6. **clear_segments()** - Remove all segments

### Memory Registry
7. **register_memory(start, end, metadata)** - Register memory region with metadata
8. **unregister_memory(id)** - Remove memory registration
9. **get_memory_at(pos)** - Get memory metadata at position

### Highlighting
10. **apply_highlighting(ranges)** - Apply batch highlighting
11. **clear_highlighting()** - Remove all highlighting

## Files to Modify
- src/text/buffer.rs (main TextBuffer struct)
- src/text/segment.rs (segment management)

## Zig Reference
Section 9 of EXISTING_OPENTUI_STRUCTURE.md: Rope-Based Text Buffers

## Design Considerations
- Segments are non-overlapping styled ranges
- Memory registry enables external tools (debuggers, profilers) to attach metadata
- Highlighting should support multiple layers (syntax, search, selection)
- All operations must maintain rope integrity

## Dependencies
- Requires ropey crate understanding
- Segment storage needs efficient range queries

## Testing Requirements
- Segment CRUD operations
- Memory registry with overlapping ranges
- Highlighting with multiple layers

 {"id":"bd-21g","title":"TextBufferView measureForDimensions","description":"## Overview
Implement measureForDimensions() to calculate required viewport size for text content.

## Scope
```rust
impl TextBufferView {
    /// Calculate the dimensions needed to display all content.
    /// Returns (virtual_line_count, max_line_width)
    pub fn measure_for_dimensions(&self, wrap_width: Option<u32>) -> (usize, usize) {
        // Use line cache if available
        // Otherwise compute on the fly
    }
}
```

## Implementation Details
- Use LineCache when wrap_width is specified and cache is valid
- For unwrapped mode, iterate lines and compute widths
- Return tuple of (total_virtual_lines, maximum_line_width)

## Files to Modify
- src/text/view.rs - add measure_for_dimensions()

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (minimum 8 tests with detailed logging)
- test_measure_no_wrap
- test_measure_with_char_wrap
- test_measure_with_word_wrap
- test_measure_empty_buffer
- test_measure_single_long_line
- test_measure_cjk_content
- test_measure_updates_after_edit
- test_measure_consistency_with_render

### Detailed Test Logging Framework
Each test MUST log dimensions and verify them:

```rust
#[test]
fn test_measure_no_wrap() {
    let mut buffer = TextBuffer::new();
    buffer.set_text("Short\nThis is a medium length line\nTiny");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Text lines:");
    for (i, line) in buffer.text().lines().enumerate() {
        eprintln!("[TEST]   Line {}: {:?} (display_width={})", 
            i, line, unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr::width(line));
    }
    
    let view = TextBufferView::new(&buffer);
    let (lines, max_width) = view.measure_for_dimensions(None);
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] measure_for_dimensions(None):");
    eprintln!("[TEST]   virtual_line_count: {}", lines);
    eprintln!("[TEST]   max_line_width: {}", max_width);
    
    assert_eq!(lines, 3, "Should have 3 lines");
    assert_eq!(max_width, 29, "Max width should be 'This is a medium length line' = 29");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: No-wrap dimensions correct");
}

#[test]
fn test_measure_with_word_wrap() {
    let mut buffer = TextBuffer::new();
    buffer.set_text("This is a line that will wrap when given a narrow width");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Text: {:?}", buffer.text());
    eprintln!("[TEST] Text length: {} chars", buffer.text().len());
    
    let view = TextBufferView::new(&buffer);
    
    // Measure with wrap width 20
    let (lines, max_width) = view.measure_for_dimensions(Some(20));
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] measure_for_dimensions(Some(20)):");
    eprintln!("[TEST]   virtual_line_count: {}", lines);
    eprintln!("[TEST]   max_line_width: {}", max_width);
    
    assert!(lines > 1, "Should wrap into multiple lines");
    assert!(max_width <= 20, "Max width should be <= wrap_width");
    
    // Measure with narrower width
    let (lines2, max_width2) = view.measure_for_dimensions(Some(10));
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] measure_for_dimensions(Some(10)):");
    eprintln!("[TEST]   virtual_line_count: {}", lines2);
    eprintln!("[TEST]   max_line_width: {}", max_width2);
    
    assert!(lines2 > lines, "Narrower width should produce more lines");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: Word wrap dimensions scale correctly");
}

#[test]
fn test_measure_cjk_content() {
    let mut buffer = TextBuffer::new();
    // Mix of ASCII and CJK (each CJK char is 2 columns)
    buffer.set_text("Hello\u{4e16}\u{754c}World");  // "Hello世界World"
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Text: {:?}", buffer.text());
    eprintln!("[TEST] Expected width: 5 + 2 + 2 + 5 = 14 columns");
    
    let view = TextBufferView::new(&buffer);
    let (lines, max_width) = view.measure_for_dimensions(None);
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Measured:");
    eprintln!("[TEST]   lines: {}", lines);
    eprintln!("[TEST]   max_width: {}", max_width);
    
    assert_eq!(lines, 1);
    assert_eq!(max_width, 14, "Width should account for double-width CJK");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: CJK character widths measured correctly");
}

#[test]
fn test_measure_empty_buffer() {
    let buffer = TextBuffer::new();
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Empty buffer");
    
    let view = TextBufferView::new(&buffer);
    let (lines, max_width) = view.measure_for_dimensions(None);
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Measured: lines={}, max_width={}", lines, max_width);
    
    assert_eq!(lines, 1, "Empty buffer should have 1 line");
    assert_eq!(max_width, 0, "Empty buffer should have 0 width");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: Empty buffer handled correctly");
}

#[test]
fn test_measure_consistency_with_render() {
    let mut buffer = TextBuffer::new();
    buffer.set_text("Line one\nLine two is longer\nLine 3");
    
    let view = TextBufferView::new(&buffer);
    
    // Measure
    let (measured_lines, measured_width) = view.measure_for_dimensions(None);
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Measured: lines={}, width={}", measured_lines, measured_width);
    
    // Render to buffer and verify dimensions match
    let mut output = OptimizedBuffer::new(measured_width as u32, measured_lines as u32);
    view.render_to(&mut output, 0, 0);
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Output buffer: {}x{}", output.width(), output.height());
    
    // Find actual content bounds
    let mut max_col = 0;
    let mut max_row = 0;
    for y in 0..output.height() {
        for x in 0..output.width() {
            if let Some(cell) = output.get(x, y) {
                if cell.content.grapheme.is_some() {
                    max_col = max_col.max(x + 1);
                    max_row = max_row.max(y + 1);
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Actual content bounds: {}x{}", max_col, max_row);
    
    assert!(max_col <= measured_width as u32);
    assert!(max_row <= measured_lines as u32);
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: Measure and render are consistent");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Returns correct line count for unwrapped text
- [ ] Returns correct line count for wrapped text
- [ ] Returns correct max width for all cases
- [ ] Handles empty buffer (returns 1, 0)
- [ ] Handles CJK/wide characters correctly
- [ ] Results are consistent with actual render output
- [ ] All 8+ unit tests pass with detailed logging
- [ ] Each test logs expected vs actual dimensions

2. Delete from boundary to cursor
3. Use Unicode word segmentation
4. Handle cursor at word boundary

- [ ] Handles cursor at boundary
- [ ] Works with Unicode text

 {"id":"bd-24dy","title":"Implement delete_word_forward() - Delete word ahead","description":"# Task: Implement delete_word_forward()

## Function Signature (Rust)
```rust
pub fn delete_word_forward(&mut self)
```

## Purpose
Deletes from cursor to end of current word (Ctrl+Delete behavior). Essential editing operation.

## Implementation Notes
1. Find word end boundary from cursor position
2. Delete from cursor to boundary
3. Use Unicode word segmentation (UAX #29)
4. Handle cursor at word boundary

## Word Boundary Rules
- Alphanumeric sequences are words
- Punctuation is typically separate
- Whitespace is separate
- Unicode categories considered

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Deletes to word end
- [ ] Uses proper word segmentation
- [ ] Handles cursor at boundary
- [ ] Works with Unicode text

## Testing
- Delete mid-word
- Delete at word start
- Delete with trailing punctuation
- Delete with Unicode (CJK, emoji)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:29:52.950909430Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:29:52.961849595Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24dy","depends_on_id":"bd-2dyx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:29:52.961610158Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
